lanciano_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Lanciano
lanciano_italy.description: In the 8th century, a Basilian monk who doubted the Real Presence saw the Host turn into real flesh and the wine into real blood during Mass. Extensive scientific testing in the 1970s concluded it is human cardiac tissue (myocardium) and AB blood type, identical to the Shroud of Turin.

bolsena_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Bolsena
bolsena_italy.description: In 1263, a doubting priest named Peter of Prague celebrated Mass. Upon breaking the Host, blood began to seep from it, staining the corporal. This miracle prompted Pope Urban IV to institute the feast of Corpus Christi.

buenos_aires_argentina.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Buenos Aires
buenos_aires_argentina.description: In 1992, 1994, and 1996, discarded Hosts began bleeding. Under the direction of Cardinal Jorge Bergoglio (later Pope Francis, who died in 2025), the tissue was scientifically analyzed and found to contain living human white blood cells and heart muscle tissue in severe distress.

sokolka_poland.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Sokółka
sokolka_poland.description: In 2008, a consecrated Host that had fallen was placed in water to dissolve. A week later, a red stain appeared on it. Pathologists concluded the substance is identical to myocardial (heart) tissue of a person nearing death.

legnica_poland.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Legnica
legnica_poland.description: In 2013, a fallen Host was placed in water. It developed red stains, which scientific analysis confirmed contained fragmented human heart muscle fibers exhibiting signs of extreme stress and agony.

tixtla_mexico.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Tixtla
tixtla_mexico.description: During a retreat in 2006, a Host began effusing a reddish substance. Scientific investigations found that the blood came from the interior of the Host outward, possessed AB blood type, and contained intact human DNA and active white blood cells.

santarem_portugal.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Santarém
santarem_portugal.description: A woman seeking to use a consecrated Host for a potion found it bleeding profusely. The Host is still preserved today in the Church of St. Stephen, having maintained its incorrupt state for over 750 years.

cascia_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Cascia
cascia_italy.description: A priest irreverently placed a consecrated Host between the pages of his breviary. The Host turned to blood, staining both pages, forming the profile of Christ’s face.

siena_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Siena
siena_italy.description: In 1730, thieves stole a ciborium containing 351 consecrated Hosts. They were recovered three days later and have since remained perfectly incorrupt and fresh, defying natural deterioration.

daroca_spain.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Daroca
daroca_spain.description: During a battle against the Moors, six consecrated Hosts were hidden in a corporal. When retrieved, they were found drenched in blood. The blood-stained corporal remains preserved as a relic.

chirattakonam_india.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Chirattakonam
chirattakonam_india.description: During Eucharistic adoration, three red stains appeared on the Host. Over the next few days, the stains formed the clear image of the face of Jesus crowned with thorns.

tumaco_colombia.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Tumaco
tumaco_colombia.description: In 1906, a massive tsunami threatened the island of Tumaco. Father Gerardo Larrondo carried the Blessed Sacrament to the shore. As a massive wave approached, it instantly receded upon reaching the Eucharist, saving the island.

amsterdam_holland.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Amsterdam
amsterdam_holland.description: A dying man received the Eucharist but vomited it up. The vomit was thrown into a roaring fire, but the Host miraculously remained unburnt and suspended in the flames.

avignon_france.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Avignon
avignon_france.description: Following severe flooding, members of the Gray Penitents rowed to their chapel expecting the Blessed Sacrament to be destroyed. Instead, they found the waters miraculously parted, leaving the altar and the Host completely dry.

krakow_poland.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Kraków
krakow_poland.description: Thieves stole a ciborium containing consecrated Hosts and dumped them in a marsh. A mysterious light led searchers to the Hosts, which were completely undamaged despite the mud.

poznan_poland.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Poznań
poznan_poland.description: Three Hosts were stolen and profaned by being pierced, causing them to bleed. They were later discovered floating in a nearby meadow, surrounded by a miraculous light.

betania_venezuela.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Betania
betania_venezuela.description: During Mass celebrated by Father Oti, the consecrated Host began to bleed profusely from one side. Medical tests confirmed the substance as human blood.

ludbreg_croatia.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Ludbreg
ludbreg_croatia.description: A priest doubting the Real Presence saw the wine turn into actual blood in the chalice. The relic of the Precious Blood is still preserved and venerated today.

walldurn_germany.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Walldürn
walldurn_germany.description: A priest accidentally overturned the chalice during Mass. The consecrated wine spilled onto the corporal, forming the image of Christ crucified and eleven identical heads of Jesus crowned with thorns.

blanot_france.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Blanot
blanot_france.description: During communion, a fragment of the Host fell to the ground. The priest tried to retrieve it, but it had turned into a drop of blood that soaked into the cloth and permanently stained it.

fiecht_austria.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Fiecht
fiecht_austria.description: In 1310, a priest doubting the Real Presence during Mass saw the consecrated wine boil and turn into blood, which is still preserved as a relic in the monastery of Fiecht.

seefeld_austria.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Seefeld
seefeld_austria.description: In 1384, a knight demanded to receive a larger Host during Mass. As he received it, the ground sank under him, and the Host turned to bleeding flesh. The relic is kept in the parish church of St. Oswald.

weiten_raxendorf_austria.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Weiten-Raxendorf
weiten_raxendorf_austria.description: In 1411, a stolen consecrated Host was hidden under a bridge. When it was recovered, the Host had turned into bleeding flesh and was emitting a supernatural light, leading to the construction of a chapel.

bois_seigneur_isaac_belgium.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Bois-Seigneur-Isaac
bois_seigneur_isaac_belgium.description: In 1405, a priest had a vision of Christ during Mass, and the Host began to bleed onto the corporal, leaving a permanent stain of blood that is still venerated today.

bruges_belgium.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Bruges
bruges_belgium.description: In 1203, the relic of the Precious Blood of Jesus, brought back from the Second Crusade, was witnessed turning into liquid blood, which is preserved in the Basilica of the Holy Blood.

brussels_belgium.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Brussels
brussels_belgium.description: In 1370, several consecrated Hosts were stolen and profaned by being stabbed with knives. The Hosts miraculously bled, leading to the recovery and veneration of the relics.

herentals_belgium.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Herentals
herentals_belgium.description: In 1412, stolen Hosts were hidden in a field. They were found weeks later completely intact, protected by wild animals, and emitting a soft light.

herkenrode_hasselt_belgium.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Herkenrode-Hasselt
herkenrode_hasselt_belgium.description: In 1317, a priest touched a consecrated Host with dirty hands, and it began to bleed, staining the altar cloth. The relic is kept in the Cathedral of St. Quentin.

liege_belgium.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Liège
liege_belgium.description: The miracle of Liège inspired St. Juliana to advocate for the feast of Corpus Christi. Consecated Hosts bled during adoration, leading to the establishment of the solemnity.

middleburg_lovanio_belgium.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Middleburg-Leuven
middleburg_lovanio_belgium.description: In 1374, a servant received communion irreverently, and the Host turned into bleeding flesh, sticking to the roof of his mouth. The relic is kept in Leuven.

st_mary_of_egypt_egypt.title: Eucharistic Miracle of St. Mary of Egypt
st_mary_of_egypt_egypt.description: In the 4th/5th century, the repentant St. Mary of Egypt was miraculously enabled to cross the Jordan River to receive the Holy Eucharist from Abbot Zosimas, surviving for decades in the desert.

scete_egypt.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Scete
scete_egypt.description: In the early desert monasteries of Scete, a monk doubting the Real Presence was shown the Host turning into a bleeding child, confirming the reality of Christ\

bordeaux_france.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Bordeaux
bordeaux_france.description: In 1822, during Eucharistic adoration, Jesus appeared inside the monstrance for over twenty minutes, blessing the congregation assembled in the chapel.

dijon_france.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Dijon
dijon_france.description: In 1430, a consecrated Host was stabbed with a knife and began to bleed. The Host was later presented to Pope Julius II and became a symbol of devotion in Dijon.

douai_france.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Douai
douai_france.description: In 1254, a priest saw a beautiful child appear in the Host during Mass. The miracle was witnessed by hundreds of parishioners who saw Jesus in the Eucharist.

faverney_france.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Faverney
faverney_france.description: In 1608, a fire broke out in the chapel, but the monstrance containing the Blessed Sacrament remained suspended in mid-air over the flames for 33 hours until the fire was extinguished.

la_rochelle_france.title: Eucharistic Miracle of La Rochelle
la_rochelle_france.description: In 1461, a mute child was healed of his muteness after receiving communion at the cathedral of La Rochelle, demonstrating the power of the Blessed Sacrament.

neuvy_saint_sepulcre_france.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Neuvy Saint Sepulcre
neuvy_saint_sepulcre_france.description: In 1257, a consecrated Host bled when a priest dropped it during Mass. The blood-stained stone and Host have been preserved in the church.

les_ulmes_france.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Les Ulmes
les_ulmes_france.description: In 1668, the face of Jesus appeared inside the Host during adoration on the feast of Corpus Christi. The apparition lasted for several hours in front of the parish.

marseille_en_beauvais_france.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Marseille-en-Beauvaisis
marseille_en_beauvais_france.description: In 1533, thieves stole a monstrance but left the consecrated Host hidden in a field. The Host was found emitting a brilliant light and was recovered in perfect condition.

paris_france_1290.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Paris
paris_france_1290.description: In 1290, a profaner stabbed a consecrated Host with a knife, throwing it into boiling water. The Host floated in the air and bled, leading to the construction of a church on the site.

pressac_france.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Pressac
pressac_france.description: In 1643, a fire destroyed the parish altar, but the consecrated Host remained completely intact and suspended above the ashes, untouched by the flames.

augsburg_germany.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Augsburg
augsburg_germany.description: In 1194, a woman kept the Host in her mouth and took it home. Years later, it was found that the Host had transformed into bleeding flesh and was preserved.

benningen_germany.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Benningen
benningen_germany.description: In 1216, a bleeding Host was found in the church of Benningen. The Host bled onto the altar, and the relic is still venerated in the parish church.

bettbrunn_germany.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Bettbrunn
bettbrunn_germany.description: In 1125, a shepherd stole a Host to protect his flock. When he dropped it, the Host stuck to a stone and could not be moved until the bishop arrived, leading to a pilgrimage.

erding_germany.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Erding
erding_germany.description: In 1417, a peasant stole a Host. As he tried to escape, the Host slipped from his hand and hovered in the air, radiating light until recovered by the clergy.

kranenburg_germany.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Kranenburg
kranenburg_germany.description: In 1280, a priest dropped a consecrated Host into a hollow in a tree. Years later, a wooden crucifix carved from the tree began to bleed, leading to a major pilgrimage.

regensburg_germany.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Regensburg
regensburg_germany.description: In 1255, a priest doubted the Real Presence. During Mass, the wooden crucifix on the altar came to life, and Jesus took the chalice from the priest\

wilsnack_germany.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Wilsnack
wilsnack_germany.description: In 1383, a church was destroyed by fire, but three consecrated Hosts survived completely intact on the altar, covered in drops of fresh blood.

weingarten_germany.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Weingarten
weingarten_germany.description: The monastery of Weingarten preserves the relic of the Precious Blood of Jesus, which was verified by several miracles and has been venerated since the 11th century.

morne_rouge_martinique.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Morne-Rouge
morne_rouge_martinique.description: In 1902, during the volcanic eruption of Mount Pelée, the sacred Host inside the monstrance showed the face of Jesus crowned with thorns in front of the fleeing population.

saint_andre_reunion.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Saint-André
saint_andre_reunion.description: In 1902, during adoration of the Blessed Sacrament, the face of Jesus and a crown of thorns appeared inside the Host, witnessed by the parish priest and the congregation.

alatri_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Alatri
alatri_italy.description: In 1228, a young woman took the Host home in a cloth. Three days later, she found that the Host had transformed into bleeding flesh, which is preserved in Alatri Cathedral.

assisi_clara_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Assisi
assisi_clara_italy.description: In 1240, St. Clare of Assisi turned back invading Saracen soldiers by holding up the monstrance containing the Blessed Sacrament at the gates of the convent.

asti_italy_1535.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Asti
asti_italy_1535.description: In 1535, a priest celebrating Mass broke the Host, and blood began to flow from the break, filling the chalice and staining the corporal and altar.

bagno_di_romagna_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Bagno di Romagna
bagno_di_romagna_italy.description: In 1412, a priest doubting the Real Presence saw the consecrated wine boil and overflow the chalice as blood, staining the corporal, which remains preserved.

canosio_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Canosio
canosio_italy.description: In 1630, a priest stopped a devastating flood of the Maira torrent by blessing the rising waters with the Blessed Sacrament, which instantly receded.

cava_dei_tirreni_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Cava de\
cava_dei_tirreni_italy.description: In 1656, a devastating plague was halted when the abbot carried the Blessed Sacrament in procession, blessing the town from the hill of Castle Saint Adiutore.

dronero_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Dronero
dronero_italy.description: In 1631, a massive fire threatened to consume the town of Dronero. A priest held up the Blessed Sacrament, and the flames immediately died out, saving the town.

san_mauro_la_bruca_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of San Mauro La Bruca
san_mauro_la_bruca_italy.description: In 1969, thieves stole a ciborium but threw away 63 consecrated Hosts. The Hosts were recovered in perfect condition and have remained incorrupt ever since.

ferrara_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Ferrara
ferrara_italy.description: In 1171, a priest celebrating Mass saw the Host turn into blood, spraying the vault above the altar with red drops, which are still visible today.

florence_italy_1230.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Florence
florence_italy_1230.description: In 1230, a priest left a few drops of consecrated wine in the chalice. The next day, the wine had turned into blood. The relic is preserved in the church of Sant\

gruaro_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Gruaro
gruaro_italy.description: In 1294, a woman washing the altar linen of the church of Gruaro found it stained with blood coming from a consecrated Host, which is preserved in Valvasone.

macerata_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Macerata
macerata_italy.description: In 1356, a priest doubting the Real Presence saw blood gush from the Host when he broke it, staining the corporal, which is still venerated in Macerata.

mogoro_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Mogoro
mogoro_italy.description: In 1604, two sinners received communion but spat the Hosts onto the stone floor, where the Hosts left deep, bleeding imprints that can still be seen today.

morrovalle_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Morrovalle
morrovalle_italy.description: In 1560, a fire completely destroyed the church, but the consecrated Host was found intact under the ashes ten days later, radiating a soft light.

offida_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Offida
offida_italy.description: In 1273, a woman threw a Host into a fire. The Host transformed into bleeding flesh and remained untouched by the flames. The relic is kept in the church of St. Augustine.

patierno_naples_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Patierno
patierno_naples_italy.description: In 1772, stolen Hosts were hidden in a field. They were found emitting a bright light and were recovered in perfect condition, accompanied by multiple miracles.

rimini_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Rimini
rimini_italy.description: In 1227, St. Anthony of Padua proved the Real Presence to an heretic when a hungry mule knelt down to adore the Blessed Sacrament instead of eating oats.

rome_italy_600.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Rome
rome_italy_600.description: In the 6th century, Pope Gregory the Great saw a consecrated Host turn into visible flesh to cure the doubt of a woman who had baked the bread.

rosano_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Rosano
rosano_italy.description: In 1948, the face of Jesus appeared inside the Host during adoration in the monastery of Rosano, witnessed by the nuns and parishioners.

peter_damian_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of St. Peter Damian
peter_damian_italy.description: In the 11th century, St. Peter Damian documented a miracle where a woman stole a Host. The Host bled onto the cloth, and when shown to him, he verified its authenticity.

salzano_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Salzano
salzano_italy.description: In 1517, a priest celebrating Mass saw the Host bleed, staining the corporal and the altar, demonstrating the Real Presence to the entire congregation.

scala_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Scala
scala_italy.description: In 1732, during adoration of the Blessed Sacrament, the instruments of the Passion (cross, crown of thorns, nails) appeared inside the Host, witnessed by St. Alphonsus Liguori.

trani_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Trani
trani_italy.description: In the 11th century, an heretic woman fried a consecrated Host in oil. The Host began to bleed profusely, overflowing the pan and spilling onto the floor.

turin_italy_1453.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Turin (1453)
turin_italy_1453.description: In 1453, a stolen monstrance containing a Host fell from a donkey. The Host rose into the air, radiating light, and remained suspended until the bishop arrived.

veroli_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Veroli
veroli_italy.description: In 1570, during adoration, the face of Jesus and a cross appeared inside the Host, witnessed by many townspeople and verified by ecclesiastical authorities.

volterra_italy.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Volterra
volterra_italy.description: In 1472, a soldier stole a pyx containing a Host. As he tried to throw it away, the Host hovered in the air and bled, leading to the conversion of the soldier.

alkmaar_netherlands.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Alkmaar
alkmaar_netherlands.description: In 1429, a priest spilled consecrated wine onto his vestments. The wine turned to blood and could not be washed out. The blood-stained vestments are preserved.

bergen_netherlands.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Bergen
bergen_netherlands.description: In 1421, a flood destroyed the church, and the consecrated Hosts were swept away. They were later found intact, with the water around them keeping them dry.

boxmeer_netherlands.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Boxmeer
boxmeer_netherlands.description: In 1400, a priest doubted the Real Presence. During Mass, a drop of blood fell from the Host into the chalice, and the wine turned to blood and overflowed.

boxtel_netherlands.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Boxtel
boxtel_netherlands.description: In 1380, a priest knocked over the chalice, spilling consecrated wine onto the altar cloth. The wine turned into blood and formed the image of Christ.

breda_netherlands.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Breda
breda_netherlands.description: In 1300, a consecrated Host was hidden in a field to prevent profanation. It was found years later completely intact and radiating a soft light.

meerssen_netherlands.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Meerssen
meerssen_netherlands.description: In 1222, a priest celebrant saw the Host bleed during Mass. In 1465, a fire destroyed the church, but the Host was found intact on the altar, untouched.

stiphout_netherlands.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Stiphout
stiphout_netherlands.description: In 1342, a fire destroyed the church, but a local peasant rushed inside and retrieved the Blessed Sacrament, which was untouched by the flames.

eten_peru.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Eten
eten_peru.description: In 1649, the face of the Child Jesus and three white hearts appeared inside the Host during adoration, witnessed by the priest and the congregation.

glotowo_poland.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Glotowo
glotowo_poland.description: In 1290, a stolen Host was buried in a field. It was found years later when oxen knelt down in front of it, and the Host was found emitting a bright light.

alboraya_spain.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Alboraya-Almácera
alboraya_spain.description: In 1348, a priest carrying communion to the sick fell into a river, losing the Hosts. The Hosts were found in the mouths of fish who held them up, waiting to return them.

alcala_spain.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Alcalá
alcala_spain.description: In 1597, a penitent returned stolen Hosts. The Hosts were kept in a drawer for years and remained completely fresh and incorrupt, verified by theologians.

alcoy_spain.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Alcoy
alcoy_spain.description: In 1568, a thief stole a ciborium. The Hosts were recovered after a local resident had a vision of where they were hidden, and they were found intact.

caravaca_spain.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Caravaca de la Cruz
caravaca_spain.description: In 1231, two angels appeared carrying a double-armed cross (the Cross of Caravaca) during Mass celebrated by a priest in front of Moorish captors.

cimballa_spain.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Cimballa
cimballa_spain.description: In 1370, a priest doubted the Real Presence. During Mass, the Host began to bleed onto the corporal, leaving a permanent stain of blood that is still venerated.

gerona_spain.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Gerona
gerona_spain.description: In 1297, a priest celebrating Mass saw the Host bleed. The blood stained the corporal, and the relic was preserved and venerated in the Cathedral of Girona.

escorial_spain.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Gorkum-El Escorial
escorial_spain.description: In 1572, Protestant soldiers profaned a consecrated Host by stepping on it. The Host bled, and the relic was later given to King Philip II and kept in El Escorial.

guadalupe_spain_1420.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Guadalupe
guadalupe_spain_1420.description: In 1420, during Mass, the Host was seen to turn into bleeding flesh, and the wine turned to blood, filling the chalice, witnessed by King John II of Castile.

ivorra_spain.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Ivorra
ivorra_spain.description: In 1010, a priest celebrating Mass doubted the Real Presence. The wine turned to blood, overflowing the chalice and staining the altar cloth, which is preserved.

moncada_spain.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Moncada
moncada_spain.description: In 1392, a priest saw the Child Jesus appear in the Host during Mass, and the Host bled, confirming the Real Presence to the congregation.

montserrat_spain_1657.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Montserrat
montserrat_spain_1657.description: In 1657, during Mass, the face of Jesus appeared inside the Host, and a series of miracles occurred in the monastery, leading to increased devotion.

cebreiro_spain.title: Eucharistic Miracle of O\
cebreiro_spain.description: In 1300, a peasant climbed the mountain in a snowstorm to attend Mass. The doubting priest saw the Host turn to flesh and the wine to blood, staining the corporal.

onil_spain.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Onil
onil_spain.description: In 1824, a stolen Host was recovered after a woman saw a mysterious light in a field. The Host was found intact and has remained incorrupt ever since.

ponferrada_spain.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Ponferrada
ponferrada_spain.description: In 1533, a thief stole a tabernacle with Hosts. The tabernacle was found hidden in a field, and the Hosts were found intact and emitting a soft light.

sant_joan_de_les_abadesses_spain.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Sant Joan de les Abadesses
sant_joan_de_les_abadesses_spain.description: In 1251, during a liturgical ceremony, the Host inside the monstrance was seen to bleed, staining the monstrance, which was preserved.

silla_spain.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Silla
silla_spain.description: In 1907, a priest dropped a consecrated Host. The Host was recovered and placed in a drawer. Years later, it was found completely intact and incorrupt.

valencia_spain.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Valencia
valencia_spain.description: The Cathedral of Valencia preserves the Holy Chalice of the Last Supper, which has been verified by numerous historic records and papal visits.

zaragoza_spain_1427.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Zaragoza
zaragoza_spain_1427.description: In 1427, a consecrated Host was stolen and hidden in a house. The Host was found emitting a bright light and was recovered in perfect condition.

ettiswil_switzerland.title: Eucharistic Miracle of Ettiswil
ettiswil_switzerland.description: In 1447, a stolen Host was buried in a field. It was found when oxen refused to step on the spot, and the Host was found hovering above the ground.

oak_cliff_us.title: Bleeding Host of Oak Cliff
oak_cliff_us.description: A consecrated host appeared to bleed. The diocese determined after an investigation that no miracle had occurred.

south_st_paul_us.title: Discolored Host of South St. Paul
south_st_paul_us.description: In the process of dissolving a discarded consecrated host in water, red coloration was noticed. The Archdiocese found the cause to be fungus.

kearns_us.title: Bleeding Host of Kearns
kearns_us.description: A consecrated host appeared to bleed. An investigation by the Diocese of Salt Lake City concluded that red bread mold naturally caused the coloration.

buffalo_us.title: Red Coloration of Host in Buffalo
buffalo_us.description: A red coloration was noticed on a host. Bishops of the Diocese of Buffalo explicitly declined to investigate.

morris_us.title: Spotted Hosts of Morris
morris_us.description: Spots thought to be blood were found on two discarded consecrated hosts. Laboratory analysis showed that these were caused by natural bacteria.
